title: scleranthus fasciculatus
intro: Scleranthus fasciculatus, also known as buttonweed or cluster cudweed, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of scleranthus fasciculatus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Scleranthus fasciculatus can be prepared for herbal use in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions.
